citation: Alain and Bengio, What Regularized Auto-Encoders Learn from the Data-Generating Distribution
primary contribution: Shows that the reconstruction displacement of a regularized autoencoder estimates a density score under stated limits
relation to revision: Bounds the manifold/vector-field interpretation used by both studies

citation: Song and Ermon, Generative Modeling by Estimating Gradients of the Data Distribution
primary contribution: Uses noise-conditional score estimation and annealed Langevin dynamics for generative modeling
relation to revision: Places the denoising intuition in the score-modeling lineage without claiming equivalence

citation: Gregor and LeCun, Learning Fast Approximations of Sparse Coding
primary contribution: Learns a fixed-depth sparse-inference network inspired by iterative optimization
relation to revision: Clarifies algorithm unrolling and differentiation through finite inference

citation: Mairal et al., Convex and Network Flow Optimization for Structured Sparsity
primary contribution: Develops scalable optimization for general overlapping group penalties
relation to revision: Provides context for overlapping group structure and proximal inference

citation: Jenatton et al., Proximal Methods for Hierarchical Sparse Coding
primary contribution: Develops exact proximal methods for tree-structured sparse coding
relation to revision: Separates prescribed structure from learned group membership

citation: Zhang et al., Beyond a Gaussian Denoiser
primary contribution: Introduces residual CNN denoising across noise settings
relation to revision: Shows why the patch-SNR comparison is not a modern denoising benchmark

primary contribution: Shows that image restoration can be learned from paired corrupted observations under statistical assumptions
relation to revision: Broadens the distinction between denoising as an evaluation task and as a learning signal

citation: Sreenivasan and Fiete, Grid Cells Generate an Analog Error-Correcting Code
primary contribution: Analyzes the precision and noise robustness of periodic population codes
relation to revision: Primary predecessor for the analog error-correction framing

citation: Wei, Prentice, and Balasubramanian, A Principle of Economy Predicts the Functional Architecture of Grid Cells
primary contribution: Derives grid lattice and module-scale predictions from neuron economy
relation to revision: Adds a principled multi-module comparison absent from the one-module model

citation: Stemmler, Mathis, and Herz, Connecting Multiple Spatial Scales to Decode the Population Activity of Grid Cells
primary contribution: Develops population-vector decoding across noisy grid modules
relation to revision: Clarifies why multiple modules resolve periodic ambiguity

citation: Yoon et al., Specific Evidence of Low-Dimensional Continuous Attractor Dynamics in Grid Cells
primary contribution: Finds low-dimensional population constraints consistent with continuous-attractor dynamics
relation to revision: Separates biological evidence from model assumption

citation: Cueva and Wei, Emergence of Grid-Like Representations by Training Recurrent Neural Networks
primary contribution: Trains recurrent networks for 2D localization and observes grid-like response patterns
relation to revision: Shows a later route toward learning rather than prescribing spatial codes

citation: Banino et al., Vector-Based Navigation Using Grid-Like Representations in Artificial Agents
primary contribution: Finds grid-like units in trained navigation agents and tests their role in vector navigation
relation to revision: Connects trained spatial representations to navigation behavior

citation: Gardner et al., Toroidal Topology of Population Activity in Grid Cells
primary contribution: Demonstrates toroidal topology in large simultaneously recorded grid-cell populations
relation to revision: Provides direct evidence relevant to the manifold/attractor framing
